title: Polymer Melt Flow Index and Viscosity Temperature Dependence
content: Melt flow index (MFI, g/10 min) characterises thermoplastic processability under ASTM D1238: 2.16 kg load at material-specific temperature (190 °C for PE, 230 °C for PP). Viscosity follows Arrhenius relationship: η = A·exp(E_a/RT) with E_a = 40–80 kJ/mol for commodity polymers. MFI < 2 indicates high viscosity (sheet/pipe extrusion); MFI > 20 indicates low viscosity (injection moulding). Thermal degradation onset detectable as colour change (yellowindex) above critical temperature; for PP onset at 260 °C.
